revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:هوفو تينماجو]][[en:Hōfu Tenmangū]][[fr:Hōfu Tenman-gū]][[ja:防府天満宮]][[zh:防府天滿宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Hōfu Tenman-gū
防府天満宮 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hofu-tenmangu1.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|47.8|N|131|34|26.6|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenmangū|Tenman-gū]] | deity = {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|qid=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}} | founded_by = | established = 904 | date_destroyed = | location = 14-1, Matsuzaki-cho, {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|qid=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}
{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}} 747-0029 | website = {{URL|http://www.hofutenmangu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Hōfu Tenman-gū'''|防府天満宮|}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|qid=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web |last=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),防府市歴史用語集,デジタル大辞泉プラス,デジタル大辞泉,事典・日本の観光資源,世界大百科事典内言及 |title=防府天満宮(ほうふてんまんぐう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E5%AE%AE-680038 |access-date=2023-01-27 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} It is one of the main shrines dedicated to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|qid=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}, the deified form of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}.{{Cite web |title=【山口】日本三大天満宮のひとつ防府天満宮を紹介します｜Stayway |url=https://stayway.jp/tourism/hofutenmangu |access-date=2023-01-27 |website=stayway media |language=ja}} [[File:Matsuzaki Tenjin engi (Hofu Tenmangu).jpg|left|thumb|Detail of a scroll painting showing Matsuzaki Shrine in Hōfu Tenmangū]] ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hofu Tenmangu Shrine (防府天満宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Hofu-tenmangu, roumon-1.jpg|280px]]
楼門 | location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}} {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|qid=Q502209|lt=Hofu|1=Hōfu}} Matsuzaki-cho 14-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|3|47.8|N|131|34|26.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | established = {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi (era)|1=Engi (era)}} 4 (904) | annual_festival = {{ill|December 5|en|December 5|qid=Q2300|lt=December 5|1=December 5}} | main_hall_style = Irimoya-zukuri | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane Pilgrimage Twenty-Five Shrines|ja|菅公聖蹟二十五拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane Pilgrimage Twenty-Five Shrines|1=Twenty-five Sacred Sites of Sugawara no Michizane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209329}} | shrine_events = | map = {{location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=防府天満宮|coordinates= {{coord2|34|3|47.8|N|131|34|26.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|3|47.8|N|131|34|26.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Hofu-tenmangu, otorii-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|First Torii Gate (Yamaguchi Prefecture Designated Cultural Property)]] '''Hōfu Tenmangū Shrine''' (防府天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|qid=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine (Japan)|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and it is now listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Overview == [[File:Hofu Tenmangu & Japanese apricot.JPG|thumb|View of the Roumon gate from the shrine's plum orchard]] The shrine was established in {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi (era)|1=Engi (era)}} 4 (904), the year after {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}'s death. It claims to be the "first Tenjin shrine built in Japan." Previously known as "Matsuzaki Tenmangu" or "Miyamachi Tenmangu," it was renamed "Matsuzaki Shrine" in 1873 under the modern shrine Ranking system and later renamed "Hōfu Tenmangū" in 1953. The shrine commemorates a location where Michizane is said to have stayed during his journey to exile in {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|qid=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}}. Along with {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|en|Kitano Tenmangū|qid=Q662176|lt=Kitano Tenmangu|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} in Kyoto and {{ill|Dazaifu Tenmangū|en|Dazaifu Tenmangū|qid=Q710786|lt=Dazaifu Tenmangu|1=Dazaifu Tenmangū}}, it is one of the {{ill|Three Great Tenjin Shrines of Japan|ja|日本三大天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Tenjin Shrines of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Three Great Tenjin Shrines|qid=Q11507109}}. Hōfu City has developed around the shrine, attracting numerous visitors for its festivals and rituals. During the New Year's holiday, the shrine receives around 300,000 visitors. Notable events include the "Ox Replacement Ritual" (牛替神事) in February and the "Divine Procession Festival" (御神幸祭) in November, also known as the "Hadaka Bō Festival" (裸坊祭). The latter involves pulling a roughly 1-ton sacred object called "Oajiro" down and back up the main stairway, often resulting in injuries. The shrine also celebrates Michizane's birth with the "Gotanshin Festival" (御誕辰祭) from August 3 to 5, which features candle-lit decorations and a fireworks display. Visitors often buy charms for exams or writing tools, as Michizane is regarded as the god of learning. In spring, the shrine's plum blossoms bloom, decorating the grounds similarly to {{ill|Dazaifu Tenmangū|en|Dazaifu Tenmangū|qid=Q710786|lt=Dazaifu Tenmangu|1=Dazaifu Tenmangū}}. The western side of the grounds features "Shunpurō," a tower-style hall originally intended as part of a five-story pagoda construction project initiated by {{ill|Mōri Narihiro|ja|毛利斉熙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Narihiro|1=Mōri Narihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11547648}} in 1822. Although the pagoda was never completed, the hall was finished in 1873 and is now used for events. The current shrine buildings were designed by {{ill|Shinzō Sugiyama|ja|杉山信三|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzō Sugiyama|1=Sugiyama Nobuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q121355467}}, under the supervision of {{ill|Jirō Murata|ja|村田治郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirō Murata|1=Jirō Murata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523473}}, after the previous structure burned down in 1952. The new structures were completed in 1958 and registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579194}} in 2009. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Paper and colored silk scroll, "Matsuzaki Tenjin Engi Emaki" (6 volumes), 1311 * Wooden seated statue of {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}, Heian period (11th century) * Gilded bronze stupa, 1172 * Asagi-ito odoshi armor, Muromachi period (15th century) === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Heiden * Shunpurō Tower (registered in 2012) == Access == === By Train === * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Hōfu Station|en|Hōfu Station|qid=Q5357631|lt=Hōfu Station|1=Hōfu Station}} on the {{ill|San'yō Main Line|en|San'yō Main Line|qid=Q1193436|lt=Sanyō Main Line|1=San'yō Main Line}} === By Car === * 10 minutes from {{ill|Hōfu Nishi Interchange|ja|防府西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfu Nishi Interchange|1=Hofu-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657013}} or {{ill|Hōfu Higashi Interchange|ja|防府東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfu Higashi Interchange|1=Hofu-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657010}} on the {{ill|San'yō Expressway|en|San'yō Expressway|qid=Q7413195|lt=Sanyō Expressway|1=San'yō Expressway}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.hofutenmangu.com/ Hōfu Tenmangū Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Shinto stub}} ==See also== {{commons category|Hōfu Tenman-gū}} *{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} *[[Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenman-gū]] *[[Tenmangū|Three Great Tenjin Shrines]] == References == {{Reflist}} ==External links== * [http://www.hofutenmangu.or.jp/ Hofu Tenmangu] (Official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|防府天満宮|version=102012072|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:防府天満宮]] [[en:Hōfu Tenmangū]] [[arz:هوفو تينماجو]] {{Commons category|Hōfu Tenman-gū}} [[fr:Hōfu Tenman-gū]] [[zh:防府天滿宮]] [[arz:هوفو تينماجو]] {{Commons category|Hōfu Tenman-gū}} [[fr:Hōfu Tenman-gū]] [[zh:防府天滿宮]] {{wikidata link|Q704898}} [[Category:10th-century establishments in Japan]] Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 1958]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]
